visa_types.name: Visa Type C (Tourism, up to 90 days within 6 months)
visa_types.documents_required: Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/aOyrGtQLI1MCamD2jTJxe/c31d00b3a9f31ae2e077f8a1efb1577b/new_checklist_type_c_greece_may.pdf) to download the checklist
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for applications is maximum 20 calendar days, as per the Schengen Regulation, In exceptional cases, this period may be extended up to 30 calendar days, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
visa_types.forms:
Please fill the form, completing all details of the applicant and submit it along with the required documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Please note that , Form Should be fill only in English. You will have to create an account to be able to fill the form. Please have your documents ready before you start filling the form.
If you face any error, please clear the cache of the browser, and retry. 
Schengen visa application form (https://www.vfsvisaonline.com/OnlineVAF-Greece?Country=oLwE+XopZo+jvCSFRX0tVWIENzdua6DWvRl5b2wKn5A8ltZGm8Wm+uayDcMXNCvU)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A tourist visa allows you to stay in the Schengen area for a short period
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for issuing the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- The Embassy of the country of first entry, if the traveler is to stay in different Schengen countries for the same amount of time.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience
Please note:
Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a Greece visa if they have at least 6 months long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A tourist visa allows you to stay in the Schengen area for a short period
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for issuing the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- The Embassy of the country of first entry, if the traveler is to stay in different Schengen countries for the same amount of time.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience
Please note:
Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a Greece visa if they have at least 6 months long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.

## Visa Fees

[grc > irn > en > Table:Visa Type C (Tourism, up to 90 days within 6 months) > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable payable visa fees are only in Euro.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of Euro 27 per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.
Visa Waiver:
- The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Family members of Greek/EU citizens;
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports;
- school pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training;
- researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations

## Documents Required
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/aOyrGtQLI1MCamD2jTJxe/c31d00b3a9f31ae2e077f8a1efb1577b/new_checklist_type_c_greece_may.pdf) to download the checklist

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the  ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain white  background.
- The photo must be no  more than 6-months old.
- Portrait-style  picture ( no slopping or posing), showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head  and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–90% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus  and clear.
Photo Size
- The picture  dimensions must be at least 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A  smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320  pixels is not valid.
- The photo should keep  a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. (i.e. 250 x 340 pixels  picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one).
- The image quality  (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An image resolution  is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing  quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution  should be 300 dpi. When you scan the image, check this parameter carefully.  However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format  accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format). The other image formats  such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected.
Attaching the Photo  to the Application form 
- Glue one photograph  to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents  and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, a  separate application has to be submitted for each child.
							   Please follow these instructions carefully. If the  photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be  considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is maximum 20 calendar days, as per the Schengen Regulation, In exceptional cases, this period may be extended up to 30 calendar days, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.

## Download Forms
Please fill the form, completing all details of the applicant and submit it along with the required documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Please note that , Form Should be fill only in English. You will have to create an account to be able to fill the form. Please have your documents ready before you start filling the form.
If you face any error, please clear the cache of the browser, and retry. 
Schengen visa application form (https://www.vfsvisaonline.com/OnlineVAF-Greece?Country=oLwE+XopZo+jvCSFRX0tVWIENzdua6DWvRl5b2wKn5A8ltZGm8Wm+uayDcMXNCvU)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A long stay visa allows you to enter Greece in order to apply for a residence permit.  This visa can be issued under various categories (i.e. work, studies, training, etc. ) and has a long-stay validity for a maximum of one year. Holders of a national visa are obligated to apply for a residence permit to Greece immediately after their first entry to the country.
Please note:
Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a Greece visa if they have at least 6 months long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A long stay visa allows you to enter Greece in order to apply for a residence permit.  This visa can be issued under various categories (i.e. work, studies, training, etc. ) and has a long-stay validity for a maximum of one year. Holders of a national visa are obligated to apply for a residence permit to Greece immediately after their first entry to the country.
Please note:
Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a Greece visa if they have at least 6 months long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.

## Visa Fees

[grc > irn > en > Table:Visa Type D (Specific Purposes Limited to Greece only) > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fees payable are only in Euro
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of Euro 27 per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.

## Documents Required

General

- Use plain white  background.
- The photo must be no  more than 6-months old.

- The picture  dimensions must be at least 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A  smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320  pixels is not valid.
- The photo should keep  a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. (i.e. 250 x 340 pixels  picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one).
- The image quality  (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An image resolution  is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing  quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution  should be 300 dpi. When you scan the image, check this parameter carefully.  However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format  accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format). The other image formats  such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected.

Note: If children are traveling with their parents  and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, a  separate application has to be submitted for each child.
							   Please follow these instructions carefully. If the  photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be  considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is maximum 30  calendar days, as per the Schengen Regulation, In exceptional cases, this period may be extended up to 45 calendar days, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
